gate_claim: after the F1 repair no checkout posture can strand the discard archive on a discarded or arbitrary branch or misreport the commit outcome: the commit lands on the surviving base branch or is truthfully reported not-committed with the receiving branch disclosed and residue recoverable, the in-place release restores the base branch despite the fresh archive on disk, the sweep behaves identically, and every previously-surviving matrix cell (commit success, diff-quiet race, commit failure, offline, preflight classification, four-edition parity) still holds
gate_surface: the release-to-discard-to-sibling-sink matrix re-run against the epoch-2 candidate with both F1 triggers (in-place NATIVE=0 release with the checkout on the feature branch; watch-pr CLOSED sweep on a non-base checkout) plus the full preflight classification matrix and the deceptive look-alike set across all four edition copies

upstream_read: n4-security-certify-fix 87ac23d2764e

verdict: fail
findings_blocking: 2

## Candidate and context

- Candidate = accumulated worktree diff vs HEAD (HEAD == claim_root_base cd28e8e52fb641cf2173ced57c91a042e3c13e1e, matching the review context). 14 modified files: the claim.js/sink-merge.js four-edition family, scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js, scripts/test-claim-hardening.js, scripts/test-sink-merge.js, CHANGELOG.md, docs/api.md, docs/workflow-state-contract.md, plus the untracked kaola-workflow/issue-715/ run tree — zero out-of-set writes. Repair examined in full: treeDirty third parameter + segment-boundary exemption with conditional -uall (claim.js:537-573), commitDiscardArchive helper with in-helper base-branch guard (:2348-2396), cmdRelease restoreExempt + releaseBaseBranch hoist + post-restore helper call + emit disclosures (:3345-3420), cmdWatchPr pre-read sweepBaseBranch + guarded call + cleanup-entry disclosures (:4346-4410), exports (:4702-4704).
- Review context honored structurally: validation_obligations [] (empty, nothing extra owed), review_phase discovery, attempt_ordinal 1, prior_findings [] — recorded. Upstream n4-security-certify-fix read in full (nonce above); my own parent-epoch refutation n4-falsify-residue-fixes (F1, triggers A/B, anchors) read from kaola-workflow/issue-715/.cache/epochs/1/files/.cache/n4-falsify-residue-fixes.md and both triggers re-run against the repaired candidate.
- Role honored read-only: no repository file modified. All fixtures were built under $TMPDIR ($TMPDIR/kw715-n5/: common.js, driver-release.js, driver-race.js, driver-watchpr.js, gh-mock.js, git shims); every cell below ran the REAL candidate claim.js end-to-end (52 + 6 + 17 = 75 executed checks). Pre-fix control materialized via `git archive HEAD scripts` into $TMPDIR, never touching the worktree.

## Both F1 triggers are genuinely repaired (executed, with pre-fix control)

- Trigger A (R1, in-place release with checkout ON the feature branch, OFFLINE=1): exit 0 released:true, restore PROCEEDED (HEAD=main), feature branch deleted, no restore_note, discard_archive_committed:true + discard_archive_branch:"main", archive tree at main:<rel>, main tip = "chore: discard archive issue-801", zero untracked residue, archive reachable from refs. The restore-gate exemption admits exactly the fresh dest and the commit lands on the surviving base. R1P pre-fix control (HEAD's claim.js, identical fixture): restore dirty-skipped (HEAD stays feature), restore_note set, NO discard_archive_committed field, archive as untracked residue — the posture delta is candidate-caused and in the right direction.
- Trigger B (W3, watch-pr CLOSED sweep on workflow/other-lane): watched:1, cleanups[0].discard_archive_committed===false + discard_archive_branch:"workflow/other-lane" + detail naming the surviving base "main", BOTH ref tips byte-unchanged, no chore commit on any ref, residue recoverable on disk AND manually committable on main afterwards. Truthful skip with disclosure and recoverable residue, exactly as claimed.

## CONFIRMED COUNTEREXAMPLE — finding N5-A (blocking): the base-branch guard accepts falsified non-surviving bases — the detached-HEAD sentinel 'HEAD' and the discarded/arbitrary current branch — committing the archive into the void and reporting committed:true, at BOTH call sites

The guard (claim.js:2363-2372) validates ONLY string equality `currentBranch === base` against a baseBranch taken verbatim from operator-controlled durable state (release: savedBaseBranch at :3384; sweep: state pre-read at :4350-4351). It never validates that base names a real surviving branch, and it accepts the literal sentinel 'HEAD' — which `rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returns exactly when NO branch is checked out. Demonstrated on the real candidate:

- B4a (release, entry posture detached HEAD, state base_branch: HEAD): restore's else-branch DELETED the feature branch (detached → not current); helper resolved currentBranch='HEAD', base='HEAD' → equal → staged + committed onto the detached HEAD; emitted {"discard_archive_committed":true,"discard_archive_branch":"HEAD"} exit 0. main never received the tree (cat-file main:<rel> → fatal). Natural cleanup (`git checkout main`) → `git rev-list --all --objects -- kaola-workflow/archive` EMPTY: the archive the release reported as committed is unreachable from every ref — the exact F1 end-state.
- W5 (watch-pr CLOSED sweep, detached HEAD, state base_branch: HEAD): identical outcome via the second call site — cleanup entry {discard_archive_committed:true, discard_archive_branch:"HEAD"}, detached tip = "chore: discard archive issue-909", main unmoved, rev-list EMPTY after checkout main.
- B3 (release, on feature branch, state base_branch: workflow/issue-801 — the discarded branch itself): restore's `git checkout workflow/issue-801` no-ops (already current), removeBranch of the current branch fails silently (pre-existing swallow), helper commits onto the discarded branch: {"discard_archive_committed":true,"discard_archive_branch":"workflow/issue-801"}, archive NOT in main; after natural cleanup (checkout main + branch -D) rev-list EMPTY.
- W6 (sweep on workflow/other-lane, state base_branch: workflow/other-lane): committed:true on the arbitrary lane, main unmoved, orphaned after lane deletion (rev-list EMPTY).
- B4b control (on feature, base_branch: HEAD): SAFE — `git checkout HEAD` no-ops on a branch (verified separately: stays on feat), guard mismatches → committed:false + disclosure + residue. The guard fails only when the sentinel comparison can actually match (detached entry) or the lie names a live non-base branch.

Precondition honestly stated: a falsified base_branch in workflow-state — the tooling itself never writes these values (claim.js:1201 and :1405 clamp baseBranch to '' for 'HEAD'/self), so a hand-edit or external corruption is required. Within that precondition the failure is total and candidate-caused: the repair's own guard — its centerpiece — accepts as "the surviving base branch" a sentinel that is by definition not a branch, and any string naming a discarded/arbitrary branch; the emit then asserts committed:true (durability) and natural cleanup orphans the archive. In this family the branch disclosure remains literally accurate; the misreport is the committed:true durability claim, and the first claim alternative ("the commit lands on the surviving base branch") is violated outright.

finding-anchor-v1:
- failure_class: logic_error (guard is pure string equality against an unvalidated operator-controlled input; accepts the rev-parse detached sentinel 'HEAD' as a branch name and any discarded/arbitrary branch name as "base")
- trigger_components: cmdRelease or cmdWatchPr CLOSED sweep with workflow-state base_branch falsified to 'HEAD', the discarded feature branch, or the current arbitrary branch; entry checkout detached or on the feature branch
- primary_anchor: scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2368 (the `currentBranch !== base` comparison — no validation that base is a real surviving branch, no rejection of the 'HEAD' sentinel)
- secondary_anchors: scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2360 (sentinel source), :2385 (post-commit check verifies presence at HEAD only, not reachability from any ref), :3384 (release passes savedBaseBranch verbatim), :4346-4351 + :4365 (sweep pre-read + call site), :3370-3372 (else-branch removeBranch deletes the feature branch while detached, completing the strand), plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js (byte twin), plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-claim.js and plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-claim.js (helper md5-identical ca78cd6993e1d37ce0048f5fe6dfecf7, call sites mirrored :3063-3102/:3509-3525 and :3058-3097/:3504-3520)
- proof: B4a/W5/B3/W6 fixture demonstrations above (emitted JSON, ref-tip evidence, rev-list --all --objects EMPTY after natural cleanup); drivers $TMPDIR/kw715-n5/driver-release.js and driver-watchpr.js
- severity: low (requires falsified durable workflow-state; the tooling never writes such a base_branch; branch disclosure stays literally accurate — but the reported committed:true is orphaned by natural cleanup, reproducing the F1 loss mode the repair exists to eliminate)
- scope: in_scope
- action: fix
- status: open
- fix_role: tdd-guide

finding: id=R1 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=base-branch guard accepts the detached-HEAD sentinel 'HEAD' and any falsified base_branch (discarded feature / arbitrary current branch) as "the surviving base" — commit binds to it, emit reports committed:true, natural cleanup orphans the archive at BOTH call sites (B4a/W5/B3/W6)

## CONFIRMED COUNTEREXAMPLE — finding N5-B (blocking): TOCTOU race between branch resolution and commit — a concurrent HEAD re-point lands the archive on an arbitrary branch while the emit reports committed:true with the stale pre-race branch name (false disclosure)

The helper resolves currentBranch once (:2360), checks it (:2368), then stages and commits (:2374-2383) and finally verifies only `cat-file -t HEAD:<rel>` (:2385) — presence at the CURRENT HEAD, never re-checking that HEAD is still the guarded branch or that the commit is reachable from base. RC1 (driver-race.js, deterministic git shim on PATH interposing at the helper's `add -A -- <rel>` call: real add, then `git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/race` + checkout): the helper's diff-quiet probe saw the staged tree, committed ONTO race, verified the tree at HEAD(=race), and the release emitted {"discard_archive_committed":true,"discard_archive_branch":"main"} — while main's tip never moved and cat-file main:<rel> → fatal "exists on disk, but not in 'main'"; race tip = "chore: discard archive issue-801". The branch disclosure is actively FALSE (not merely incomplete): the archive is stranded on an arbitrary branch and misreported as committed on the surviving base; deleting race orphans it. Precondition: a concurrent repo-mutating process inside the add→commit window — the same adversary class as the parent surface's diff-quiet-race cell (which the repair handles truthfully — RC2 below), so concurrent git mutation is in-scope for this claim; and the branch-switch variant is the directed "race between branch resolution and staging" attack. A post-commit re-resolution + reachability check (abbrev-ref re-read, merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD base, downgrade to committed:false + residue on mismatch) would convert this silent misreport into a truthful skip.

finding-anchor-v1:
- failure_class: logic_error (check-then-act TOCTOU: branch identity resolved once, never re-verified against the commit's actual destination; presence-at-HEAD verification is branch-blind)
- trigger_components: any release/sweep discard-archive commit racing a concurrent HEAD re-point (symbolic-ref/checkout) between the guard (:2360-2368) and the commit (:2383)
- primary_anchor: scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2385 (post-commit verification checks only presence at CURRENT HEAD — not that HEAD is still base nor reachability from base)
- secondary_anchors: scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2360, :2368, :2383, and the byte-identical helper in the three other edition copies (md5 ca78cd6993e1d37ce0048f5fe6dfecf7 each)
- proof: RC1 shim demonstration above — emitted JSON with discard_archive_committed:true + discard_archive_branch:"main" while mainTipMoved=false, archiveOnMain=fatal, archiveOnRace=tree, raceTipSubject="chore: discard archive issue-801"; driver $TMPDIR/kw715-n5/driver-race.js
- severity: low (requires a concurrent repo-mutating adversary in a sub-second window; but the outcome is the worst misreport class — a false receiving-branch disclosure with committed:true)
- scope: in_scope
- action: fix
- status: open
- fix_role: tdd-guide

finding: id=R2 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=TOCTOU between branch resolution and commit: concurrent HEAD re-point strands the archive commit on an arbitrary branch while emit reports committed:true with the stale pre-race branch name (RC1: reported main, actual race)

## Claim clauses that SURVIVED strong counterexample attempts (all executed)

- Restore-gate exemption scoping (A2 + TX unit pins on the exported treeDirty): foreign sibling dirt beside the dest (kaola-workflow/archive/foreign-scratch.txt AND a discarded-look-alike sibling dir) still blocks the restore → truthful skip (restore_note + committed:false + branch:workflow/issue-801 + detail + loud warning + residue recoverable, both ref tips unchanged). Unit pins: exact dest exempt (proves -uall expands the collapsed untracked tree); suffix-concat look-alike (dest2) and dash-suffix look-alike (dest-evil) NOT exempt; foreign sibling under archive/ blocks; foreign file elsewhere blocks; staged foreign change blocks; trailing-slash exemption form matches; baseline without exemption still counts archive/* dirty (never-parked intact); the two other treeDirty callers (:1158, :1353) pass no exemption and are behavior-unchanged (no -uall unless exempt non-empty).
- Honest-state guard postures: detached HEAD (B1 release, W4 sweep) → committed:false + branch:"HEAD" + not-base detail + residue, main unchanged. Symbolic-ref branch (B2): git resolves the symref checkout to main; commit landed on main, tree at main:<rel>, disclosure accurate. Lying base='HEAD' entered ON the feature branch (B4b): `git checkout HEAD` no-ops (git semantics verified separately), guard mismatches → truthful skip + residue.
- Commit success: R0 (release from main, linked-worktree posture) and W1 (CLOSED sweep on main): committed:true on main, tree at main:<rel>, chore-commit tip, zero residue.
- Diff-quiet race (RC2, epoch-1 R3 re-run with a shim committing the staged rel between add and commit): helper's own commit skipped (no "chore: discard archive" in log), race commit present exactly once, committed:true + branch:"main" with tree at main — truthful, no duplicate, no spurious failure.
- Commit failure: R2a (pre-commit hook exit 1) → exit 0 released:true (never stranded), committed:false + branch:"main" + "discard archive commit failed" detail + loud warning, no chore commit, residue staged-recoverable. R2b (.gitignore defeats add) → restore proceeded (ignored dest invisible), committed:false + "git add failed … ignored" detail + warning, residue on disk.
- Offline: every release fixture ran KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=1; grep-verified the helper body contains no OFFLINE branch — the commit that runs is never skipped for offline.
- Preflight classification surface: SINK_RECEIPT_EXEMPT regex micro-probe 21/21 as expected (single-segment live/archive match; fallback, .tmp/.bak suffix, directory form, multi-segment nested, x/.cache, case, whitespace, trailing-slash, double-slash all refuse; the two degenerate reserved-name forms still exempt = parent O1, classification-only). Shipped suite node scripts/test-sink-merge.js → 105 assertions passed including the #715 (l) over-exemption guard and (m) own-receipt regression lock; walkthrough --only testSinkForeignDirtExemptsSiblingReceipt715 passed.
- Four-edition parity (proven, not inferred): codex claim.js/sink-merge.js byte-identical twins (diff empty); commitDiscardArchive body md5 ca78cd6993e1d37ce0048f5fe6dfecf7 in canonical/gitlab/gitea; treeDirty executable body md5 d9f91cdb60ee3eb09c9889fca794095f in all three; SINK_RECEIPT_EXEMPT byte-identical in all four sink-merge copies; every call-site line (releaseBaseBranch hoist, restoreExempt, sweepBaseBranch pre-read + fallback, both helper calls, disclosures) present in both forge ports. Both findings above therefore span the full four-edition surface.
- Shipped suites re-run by this reviewer in the worktree: test-claim-hardening.js → 464 assertions passed (incl. the 14 #715 F1 pins); simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js → full pass, and --only testReleaseInPlaceOnFeatureBranchCommitsArchiveOnBase / testWatchPrClosedSweepSkipsCommitOffBaseBranch / testSinkForeignDirtExemptsSiblingReceipt715 each passed standalone; test-bundle-finalize.js → 149 passed.

## Non-blocking observations (not counted in findings_blocking)

- O-N5-1: in B3's posture the restore block's removeBranch of the CURRENT branch fails and is silently swallowed (pre-existing catch(_) at :3373) — no restore_note or warning records that the intended branch deletion did not happen; the new emit shape could have surfaced it. Pre-existing swallow, non-blocking.
- O-N5-2: parent-epoch O1 stands unchanged — the exemption regex also matches the degenerate reserved-name forms kaola-workflow/archive/.cache/sink-receipt.json and kaola-workflow/.cache/.cache/sink-receipt.json (probe cases 17-18). Classification-only, zero mutation impact, as classified in the parent epoch.

## Outcome rationale

The F1 repair is real: both parent triggers re-run green with pre-fix controls, and every previously-surviving cell (commit success, diff-quiet race, commit failure, offline, honest-state detached/symbolic-ref postures, full preflight classification matrix, deceptive look-alikes, four-edition parity, all shipped suites) held under re-attack. However the directed attacks "a base_branch field lying in workflow-state" and "a race between branch resolution and staging" each produced a concrete, executed, candidate-caused counterexample to the claim's absolute guarantee: N5-A strands the archive with committed:true through the guard's acceptance of the 'HEAD' sentinel / falsified base at both call sites (the exact F1 loss mode under a falsified-state precondition), and N5-B produces a false receiving-branch disclosure under a concurrent HEAD re-point. Both are severity low (adversarial preconditions stated precisely); per the inverted burden, demonstrated counterexamples in the directed classes refute the claim as written. Confidence: high on both findings (deterministic end-to-end demonstrations on the real candidate with reachability evidence); high on the surviving clauses (executed, not inferred).

domain_outcome: refuted
claim_outcome: refuted
